behaviors.description: Cron parser rejects invalid expressions with descriptive error message
behaviors.justification: The cron parser is a pure function that validates syntactic correctness. A real failure (wrong validation logic, missing error case) would be caught by a local unit test with parametrized invalid inputs. No external dependencies, no I/O, no interaction with other modules needed.
behaviors.test_description: Parametrized unit test covering invalid cron expressions: month 13, day 32, malformed syntax (missing fields, invalid characters). Verify each raises a specific exception (e.g., InvalidCronExpression) with descriptive error message identifying the invalid field and acceptable range. Use a table-driven approach to test 10+ invalid cases.

behaviors.description: Next-run-time calculator returns correct timestamps for standard and edge-case cron expressions
behaviors.justification: Computing future timestamps from cron + start time is a pure algorithmic operation with deterministic output. A real failure (off-by-one, wrong day-of-month logic, year boundary bug) is caught by comparing computed timestamps against known correct values. The algorithm is self-contained; no I/O or external calls needed.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test with parametrized (cron_expression, start_time, expected_next_times) tuples. Cover standard cases (e.g., '0 9 * * 1' = every Monday 9 AM) and edge cases: last day of month (day 31 in February), day-of-week + day-of-month interaction (cron's OR semantics), year boundaries (Dec 31 → Jan 1), leap years. Verify exact timestamp matches for next 3–5 runs.

behaviors.description: Overlap detector identifies conflicting schedules within the minimum gap and reports time windows
behaviors.justification: The overlap detection algorithm is a comparison operation: given two lists of fire times and a minimum gap threshold, determine which pairs are closer than the gap. This is pure logic, testable with mocked or pre-computed fire time lists. Real failures (wrong inequality, off-by-one in gap check) are caught without needing to wire the detector to the calculator or Schedule objects.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test with two pre-computed fire time lists (or fixture objects with fire_times attributes). Create pairs with overlaps at boundary conditions: exactly at minimum gap (5 min), 1 second before gap, 1 second after gap. Verify detector reports correct (time_a, time_b) pairs as conflicts and omits non-conflicting pairs. Test wrapping midnight and multiple overlaps in one window.

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling handles DST transitions (spring-forward gap and fall-back ambiguity) correctly
behaviors.justification: The real failure is in the scheduler's algorithm—how it computes next run times when a scheduled time (e.g., 2:30 AM) is ambiguous or non-existent due to DST. Testing requires real tzinfo objects (from zoneinfo or pytz) to correctly model DST rules, but no subprocess, OS manipulation, or external API calls. Using real tzinfo objects is Unit-level; mocking them would risk hiding the bug.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test of the next-run-time calculator with real tzinfo objects (e.g., zoneinfo.ZoneInfo('US/Eastern')). Given cron '30 2 * * *' (2:30 AM daily) and a start_time on a spring-forward date (gap) or fall-back date (ambiguous), verify next run time is computed correctly: e.g., skip to 3:30 AM for spring-forward, or use first/second occurrence for fall-back. Test multiple timezones (US/Eastern, Europe/London) with known DST transition dates.

behaviors.description: Missed-run policy engine determines whether to skip or backfill a scheduled job that has already fired
behaviors.justification: This is a pure decision algorithm: given a missed run time, current time, and policy (SKIP or BACKFILL), choose an action. The logic is deterministic and self-contained. Real failures (wrong policy branch, incorrect backfill calculation) are caught by injecting a fixed 'now' timestamp and verifying the engine's decision. No I/O, no external calls, no OS dependencies needed.
behaviors.test_description: Parametrized unit test with (scheduled_time, now_time, policy, expected_action) tuples. For SKIP policy, verify the engine logs/records the miss but does not execute. For BACKFILL policy, verify it queues the job for immediate execution. Test boundary: a job with scheduled_time = now (edge of miss detection). Inject mocked timestamps rather than relying on system clock.
